page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz with the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks reflect the characteristics of the Neoverse-N1 architecture. The instance achieves average performance in stress-ng single-core (1519.39) and multi-core (6055.47) CPU tests. Large block memory bandwidth reads (72.78 GB/sec) and writes (35.07 GB/sec) also fall into the average range, alongside database operations in PassMark (2626.26). However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in single-threaded gzip compression (20.32 GB/sec), Geekbench multi-core execution (3217.0), and Redis SET operations (4.88 million operations per second). Cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support, though the processor lacks AVX or AMX instruction sets.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, the c6gn.xlarge is optimized for network-bound workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture provides a lower-cost alternative to traditional x86 instances for workloads that do not require high memory-to-core ratios or specialized vector instructions. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached EBS volumes. This instance is suited for network-optimized applications, lightweight containerized microservices, static web serving, and basic database operations that fit within its 8.0 GB memory footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average performance in multi-core CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth, though it exhibits weaker results in single-threaded compression and Geekbench suites. Cryptographic tasks are supported via hardware AES, but the processor lacks AVX and AMX extensions. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for arm64-native workloads, making it suitable for network-optimized applications, static web serving, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized arm64 instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and enhanced network bandwidth for balanced, cost-efficient scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM processor at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Best suited for network-bound applications, static web serving, and lightweight databases
